name: Firmware
description: System software executing on dedicated hardware processors that provides low-level control, hardware initialization, and runtime services independent of the main operating system.
purpose: Enable platform hardware initialization, boot sequence orchestration, device management, and provision of low-level runtime services across enterprise endpoints.

name: BIOS
description: Legacy pre-boot firmware stored in non-volatile memory that executes during system power-on to initialize hardware components, perform Power-On Self-Test (POST), enumerate boot devices, and provide interrupt-based runtime services to the operating system bootloader.
purpose: Perform platform initialization during boot sequence, execute hardware diagnostics, identify bootable storage devices, and provide low-level runtime services via interrupt handlers.

name: UEFI
description: Modern pre-boot firmware specification providing 32-bit and 64-bit protected mode operation, support for GPT partition tables, embedded network stack, Secure Boot enforcement, and extensible driver model for platform initialization and runtime services.
purpose: Provide modern boot environment with enhanced security controls, support GPT-based disk partitioning, enable network-based boot, enforce Secure Boot policies, and deliver extensible pre-OS platform services.

name: Embedded Controller Firmware
description: Low-level firmware executing on a dedicated embedded microcontroller that manages board-level hardware functions including power sequencing, thermal regulation, battery charging control, keyboard matrix scanning, and fan operation independent of the main CPU.
purpose: Autonomously manage hardware power delivery, thermal policy enforcement, battery state monitoring, keyboard input processing, and generate events for the main platform firmware.

name: Storage Firmware
description: Firmware embedded within storage devices including hard disk drives, solid-state drives, and NVMe devices that manages media access operations, error correction, wear leveling algorithms, encryption operations, and interface protocol compliance independent of the host operating system.
purpose: Manage storage media access, execute hardware-based encryption, perform error correction and wear leveling, enforce protocol compliance, and optimize device performance.

name: Network Firmware
description: Firmware embedded within network interface controllers, wireless adapters, and out-of-band management controllers that governs packet processing, PHY layer operations, protocol offload functions, remote management capabilities, and preboot network services independent of host operating system.
purpose: Manage network packet processing and transmission, execute protocol offload operations, enable out-of-band platform management, support network-based boot services, and provide wireless connectivity controls.
